start.text: You approach his bed with the same smile you've been practicing since yesterday. The professional one. The one that stays up regardless.
start.speaker: empty

morning_uncle.text: Good morning, Uncle Lim! How are we feeling today?
morning_uncle.speaker: player

fixed_on_window.text: He doesn't look at you right away. He's got his eyes fixed on the window again, the same window, the same nothing outside it.
fixed_on_window.speaker: empty

run_through_checks.text: Let me run through your checks, okay? Won't take long.
run_through_checks.speaker: player

extends_arm.text: He extends his arm without a word. You wrap the cuff around it, the velcro catching the way it always does. You remember rubbing his cold hands before attaching the pulse oximeter two days ago. You don't this time. You're not sure he'd want you to.
extends_arm.speaker: empty

bp_stable.text: BP looks stable. That's good news, Uncle.
bp_stable.speaker: player

how_pain_today.text: How's the pain today? Scale of one to ten?
how_pain_today.speaker: player

same_as_yesterday.text: Same as yesterday? So around a four?
same_as_yesterday.speaker: player

sharpness.text: The sharpness of it catches you off guard. You write down four and move on.
sharpness.speaker: empty

eating_okay.text: Are you eating okay? The nurses on the night shift noted you didn't finish your dinner—
eating_okay.speaker: player

options.response: Not hungry lah. Does it matter?

body_needs.text: Uncle, it does matter. Your body needs—
body_needs.speaker: player

options.response: My body just had a whole operation done to it. Let it rest, can or not?

close_mouth.text: You close your mouth. You note it down. Appetite reduced. You stare at the words for a moment longer than necessary.
close_mouth.speaker: empty

anything_you_need.text: Uncle... is there anything you need? Anything I can—
anything_you_need.speaker: player

turns_away.text: He pulls the blanket up to his chin and turns onto his side, away from you.
turns_away.speaker: empty

three_days_ago.text: You stand there for a moment with your clipboard pressed against your chest. Three days ago he was telling you about the Jurong Shipyard and laughing about kopi o kosong. Two days ago he was squeezing your hand before surgery. Yesterday he was quietly falling apart. And today he won't even look at you.
three_days_ago.speaker: empty

which_version.text: You don't know which version of him to hold onto.
which_version.speaker: empty
